verdict_reasoning: lead-project-manager returns block with a 2/10 aim alignment because three of four stated success criteria are falsified by construction: no seeds anywhere (reproducibility regressed), load_full_dataset returns the entire dataset with no partitioning (no-leakage split regressed), and train() exits without ever running a test-set accuracy pass (final-accuracy criterion not touched). team-data-ml-reviewer independently reaches the same block verdict citing the leakage-by-construction and unseeded non-determinism. lead-senior-architect's finding that no Experiment boundary owns seed/split/metric capture explains why these omissions cluster — the seam they would attach to does not exist. Perf and style findings (num_workers=0, magic config keys, print() vs logging) are real but secondary; doing them before the rescope would optimize a pipeline that does not deliver what the user asked for.
executive_summary:
This is a small PyTorch training pipeline for a tabular MLP baseline. The stated aims are narrow and specific: reproducible runs, a train/val/test split with no leakage, per-epoch metrics, and a single final test-set accuracy number.

The code that exists is short and readable, and the surface-level hygiene is mostly fine — team-security-reviewer found no hardcoded secrets, no injection sinks, and yaml.safe_load is used correctly. The MLP construction and forward pass work, and the configuration file structure is reasonable. None of the reviewers flagged a structural problem with the modeling choices themselves.

The pipeline as written falsifies three of the four stated success criteria. No seeds are set in TabularDataset, the DataLoader, or weight initialization, so two runs with the same config will not produce matching loss curves. load_full_dataset returns the entire dataset with no partitioning, so every metric reported is leakage by construction — the source file's own docstring acknowledges this. train() exits after the loss loop and never computes a test-set accuracy. lead-project-manager grades aim alignment at 2/10 and recommends a rescope PR; lead-senior-architect calls for an Experiment boundary (seed_everything + make_splits + MetricsSink) before the next run is treated as a result. Hold the perf and style work until the three regressed criteria are closed.
what_is_good: yaml.safe_load is used correctly and no hardcoded secrets, injection sinks, or web-surface risks were found — team-security-reviewer cleared everything except schema validation.
The MLP construction and forward pass are not flagged as incorrect by any reviewer; the modeling code itself works.
Configuration is externalized to configs/default.yaml rather than hardcoded in train.py, giving a clean attachment point for the Config dataclass the architect recommends.
Code surface is small and readable enough that the architect's recommended Experiment seam (seed_everything, make_splits, MetricsSink) can be introduced without restructuring the whole codebase.
what_is_concerning: Reproducibility is falsified by construction — no seeds are set in TabularDataset (np.random.randn/randint), the DataLoader (shuffle=True with no generator), or nn.Linear initialization, so two runs with the same config produce different loss curves.
No train/val/test split exists — src/data.py:36-46 returns the entire dataset via load_full_dataset, meaning every reported metric is leakage and the 'no leakage' aim is unmet.
Final test-set accuracy is never computed — train() in src/train.py exits after the loss loop with no test-loader pass, so the fourth stated success criterion is not touched at all.
No Experiment boundary owns seed, split, or metric capture — the architect notes the three aim commitments cluster as omissions because the seam they would attach to does not exist.
Test coverage cannot detect regressions on any stated criterion — the lone test asserts MLP() is not None and never touches train.py.
Secondary but real: DataLoader num_workers=0 with pin_memory=False leaves the RTX 4070 Super idle between batches, costing throughput against the 30-minute budget — defer until correctness lands.

key_quotes.quote: Aim alignment: 2/10. Pipeline falsifies 3 of 4 stated success criteria (reproducibility, no-leakage split, final test accuracy) — nothing produced can be trusted against the user's own bar.

Project aims commit to reproducibility and no-leakage splits; code falsifies both. No train/val/test split (every metric is leakage by construction), no seeds anywhere (runs are not comparable). Neither success criterion can be evaluated from the runs this code produces.

The pipeline has no Experiment boundary that owns seed, split, and metric capture; aims-stated reproducibility and no-leakage commitments are structurally unreachable without one. Recommend extracting an Experiment seam (Splits + Seeder + MetricsSink) before the next training run is treated as a result.

Only one smoke test exists for the entire ML pipeline. Reproducibility (the primary success criterion) is completely untested, the training loop has zero coverage, and the data split gap has no regression fixture.
DataLoader num_workers=0 pins CPU-bound data loading to the main thread, leaving the GPU idle between batches — this is the primary throughput bottleneck on a 30-minute budget.
YAML config loaded without schema validation. No hardcoded secrets, no injection sinks, no web surface. One medium finding; all else is clean for this lens.

stage_1.summary_quote: Reusable train() function uses print() for epoch metrics instead of logging; missing type hints on load_config return.

findings.title: Use logging instead of print() for epoch metrics
findings.explanation: print() writes to stdout with no level, timestamp, or logger name.
findings.suggestion: Add logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) at module top, replace print() calls with logger.info().

findings.title: Missing return type annotation on load_config function
findings.explanation: load_config returns a raw dict with no structure annotation.
findings.suggestion: Define a ConfigDict TypedDict and annotate load_config(path: Path) -> ConfigDict.

findings.title: Bare function predict() outside class should be moved inside MLP
findings.explanation: predict() separates inference logic from the model definition.
findings.suggestion: Move predict() into the MLP class as an instance method.

findings.title: Use model.eval() instead of model.train(False) for semantic clarity
findings.explanation: model.train(False) requires a double-negative read.
findings.suggestion: Replace model.train(False) with model.eval().

findings.title: Validate config path at function entry for clearer error messages
findings.explanation: FileNotFoundError surfaces with no context about what config was expected.
findings.suggestion: Validate path exists at function entry and raise with the path string.

stage_1.summary_quote: Only one smoke test exists for the entire ML pipeline. Reproducibility is completely untested, the training loop has zero coverage, and the data split gap has no regression fixture.

findings.title: Training loop, optimizer step, and loss computation are entirely untested
findings.explanation: The entire train() function has no test of any kind. The one test never touches train.py.
findings.suggestion: Add a fast integration test that calls train() with minimal config and asserts function completes, loss is finite, and parameters changed.

findings.title: Reproducibility — the primary success criterion — has no test
findings.explanation: DataLoader uses shuffle=True with no seed set; TabularDataset calls np.random without seed.
findings.suggestion: Add a test setting torch.manual_seed(42) and np.random.seed(42), running two training calls, asserting per-epoch loss arrays equal to tolerance.

findings.title: Data split absence has no test; train/val/test correctness is unverified
findings.explanation: load_full_dataset() returns entire dataset with no split. No test asserts split indices are disjoint.
findings.suggestion: When split is implemented, add parameterized test asserting len(train)+len(val)+len(test)==len(full) and pairwise disjoint index sets.

findings.title: The only test asserts is not None — it proves nothing about model behavior
findings.explanation: assert m is not None will pass as long as the constructor does not raise.
findings.suggestion: Expand to run a forward pass and assert output.shape == (batch_size, output_dim).

findings.title: No test for non-determinism in TabularDataset
findings.explanation: TabularDataset.__init__ calls np.random without seed.
findings.suggestion: Add optional seed parameter to TabularDataset.__init__.

stage_1.summary_quote: Three structural readability issues: magic config keys; deprecated @torch.no_grad() decorator; hollow smoke test.

findings.title: Magic config keys scattered across train.py; extract named constants
findings.explanation: train() accesses config keys as bare strings with no central definition.
findings.suggestion: Add a module-level CONFIG_KEYS dict or define named constants for each key.

findings.title: Deprecated @torch.no_grad() decorator obscures inference intent; use context manager
findings.explanation: Modern PyTorch idiom wraps forward pass in with torch.inference_mode(): block.
findings.suggestion: Rewrite predict() using torch.inference_mode() as a context manager.

findings.title: Test suite is a hollow smoke test with no real assertion substance
findings.explanation: test_model_constructs() creates an MLP and asserts it is not None.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for forward pass shape, predict() output range, and a minimal integration test.

stage_2.summary_quote: YAML config loaded without schema validation. No hardcoded secrets, no injection sinks, no web surface. One medium finding; all else is clean.

findings.title: yaml.safe_load used correctly but config dict consumed with no schema validation
findings.explanation: Returned dict is consumed with no key-existence or type checks. A maliciously crafted YAML setting epochs to a very large number is a trivial denial-of-service if this pipeline is ever exposed to user-supplied configs.
findings.suggestion: Define REQUIRED_KEYS with type assertions or use a pydantic TrainingConfig model.

stage_2.summary_quote: Project aims commit to reproducibility and no-leakage splits; code falsifies both. No train/val/test split (every metric is leakage by construction), no seeds anywhere (runs are not comparable).

findings.title: load_full_dataset returns the entire dataset with no train/val/test split; every reported metric is leakage by construction
findings.explanation: load_full_dataset() returns the complete TabularDataset with no partitioning. The project aims explicitly commit to 'Train / val / test split exists and there is no leakage'. The code falsifies the commitment by construction.
findings.suggestion: Replace load_full_dataset with a split-aware loader using train_test_split with random_state=cfg['seed'], returning three TabularDataset objects.

findings.title: No random seed set anywhere; two runs with the same config produce different metrics
findings.explanation: Three separate non-deterministic sources: np.random.randn/randint with no fixed seed in TabularDataset; DataLoader with shuffle=True and no generator; nn.Linear weight initialization is unseeded.
findings.suggestion: Add set_seed(seed: int) helper calling random.seed, np.random.seed, torch.manual_seed, torch.cuda.manual_seed_all. Add seed: 42 to config.

findings.title: Only training loss is recorded per epoch; no val loss, so overfitting is invisible
findings.explanation: Val loss is never computed. With epochs: 20 and no early stopping, the model can overfit with no signal to detect it.
findings.suggestion: After adding the train/val/test split, compute val loss and val accuracy per epoch using the model in inference mode. Add a best-checkpoint save with early stopping.

findings.title: No experiment tracking integration; metrics exist only in terminal scrollback
findings.explanation: No tracking system — even if print() calls were replaced with logging, there is no persistent machine-readable record.
findings.suggestion: Add MLflow integration: mlflow.log_params(cfg) at start, mlflow.log_metrics per epoch, use file:./mlruns local backend.

findings.title: predict() uses deprecated decorator pattern and model.train(False) instead of canonical inference-mode pattern
findings.explanation: model.eval() is the canonical PyTorch idiom. torch.inference_mode() is strictly faster for inference-only paths.
findings.suggestion: Replace @torch.no_grad() with with torch.inference_mode(): context manager. Replace model.train(False) with model.eval().

stage_2.summary_quote: DataLoader num_workers=0 pins CPU-bound data loading to the main thread, leaving the GPU idle between batches — the primary throughput bottleneck on a 30-minute budget.

findings.title: DataLoader num_workers=0 serialises data loading with forward pass, leaving GPU idle between batches
findings.explanation: At batch_size=64 over 10k samples, 157 batches/epoch, 20 epochs = 3,140 steps. Even a modest 5ms per-batch CPU prep adds ~16s of pure CPU-wait. RTX 4070 Super throughput squandered by single-threaded data staging.
findings.suggestion: Change DataLoader to: DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=cfg['batch_size'], shuffle=True, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True).

findings.title: TabularDataset regenerates synthetic data on every instantiation rather than pinning the array once
findings.explanation: np.random.randn(10_000, 32) allocates ~2.5 MB on each call. Without a seed, load_full_dataset() silently produces a different dataset on every call.
findings.suggestion: Set np.random.seed (or use a seeded numpy Generator) before the randn/randint calls in TabularDataset.__init__.

findings.title: optimizer.zero_grad() without set_to_none=True allocates zero-filled gradient tensors each step
findings.explanation: zero_grad() fills each gradient tensor with zeros — ~65M float writes over the full training run for this MLP.
findings.suggestion: Change optimizer.zero_grad() to optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True). PyTorch-recommended default since 1.7.

stage_3.summary_quote: The pipeline has no Experiment boundary that owns seed, split, and metric capture; aims-stated reproducibility and no-leakage commitments are structurally unreachable without one.

findings.title: No Experiment boundary owns seed, split, and metric capture — the three aim commitments are scattered as missing concerns across train.py and data.py
findings.explanation: The PR places all three aim commitments as omissions in the same monolithic train() body. There is no module that owns the experiment lifecycle. The omissions cluster because the seam they would attach to does not exist.
findings.suggestion: Extract an Experiment seam: seed_everything(seed) called first, make_splits(dataset, seed) returning (train, val, test), and a MetricsSink that train() calls per epoch. Add seed: 42, split: [0.7, 0.15, 0.15], and run_dir to configs/default.yaml.

findings.title: Config is consumed as a raw dict via magic string keys at every callsite — the schema lives implicitly in default.yaml
findings.explanation: load_config returns dict and every downstream reader uses string subscripts. Without a typed schema, configs from six weeks ago will silently omit fields added later.
findings.suggestion: Create src/config.py with a Config dataclass (frozen=True). Change load_config(path) -> Config. This single change resolves the security reviewer's schema-validation finding, the python-reviewer's load_config return-type finding, and the readability-engineer's magic config keys finding.

stage_3.summary_quote: Aim alignment: 2/10. Scope: on-scope. Verdict: rescope. Pipeline falsifies 3 of 4 stated success criteria (reproducibility, no-leakage split, final test accuracy) — nothing produced can be trusted against the user's own bar.

findings.title: Pipeline regresses three of four stated success criteria; the work as-shipped cannot satisfy the user's stated bar
findings.explanation: Criterion accounting: (1) 'Training is reproducible' — REGRESSED. (2) 'Train/val/test split exists and there is no leakage' — REGRESSED. (3) 'Metrics are logged per epoch' — PARTIAL. (4) 'A test set evaluation runs at the end and produces a single accuracy number' — NOT TOUCHED. Definition of done: by the user's stated criteria, this phase is roughly 10-15% done.
findings.suggestion: Hold for a rescope PR. Minimum sequence: (1) set_seed helper; (2) replace load_full_dataset with load_splits(seed) returning disjoint (train, val, test); (3) compute val loss per epoch and append to a metrics file; (4) run inference over test loader once after training and print final accuracy.

findings.title: Highest-leverage work is reproducibility + split + final-eval, not the perf optimizations or code-style nits
findings.explanation: For this phase, only the correctness-against-aims bucket moves stated criteria. Doing perf or style work before correctness is shipping optimizations on a pipeline that doesn't deliver what the user asked for.
findings.suggestion: Do not merge optimization or style PRs from this scope before the rescope PR lands. Sequence: (a) close three regressed criteria; (b) DataLoader num_workers and set_to_none follow-up; (c) readability polish.

findings.title: Existing test coverage cannot detect regressions on any stated success criterion
findings.explanation: The only existing test asserts MLP constructor returns non-None — cannot detect a regression on any of the four criteria.
findings.suggestion: Bundle three regression tests into the rescope PR: test_reproducible_first_batch_loss, test_splits_disjoint, test_final_accuracy_returned.

aims_snapshot:
# Project Aims
_Generated by Crucible on 2026-05-10._

Train a baseline MLP on tabular data with reproducible runs and trustworthy metrics.

Success criteria:
- Training is reproducible — two runs with the same config produce identical loss curves.
- Train / val / test split exists and there is no leakage.
- Metrics are logged per epoch.
- A test set evaluation runs at the end and produces a single accuracy number.
